input.description: Research Claude Code usage reset windows
input.prompt:
The user wants to know about Claude Code's subscription /usage functionality and quota reset mechanism. Specifically: is the usage quota reset on a rolling window basis (e.g., "last 5 hours") or does it reset at fixed calendar times (e.g., every hour on the hour, or daily at midnight)?

Please research this and give a concise, accurate answer. Check documentation, source code, or any available references.

content.text:
Perfect. Now I have the official answer. Let me provide you with a concise, accurate response.

---

## Claude Code Usage Quota Reset: Rolling Window (5-Hour Basis)

**Claude Code usage quotas reset on a rolling 5-hour window, NOT on fixed calendar times.**

### Key Details:

- **Rolling window**: The 5-hour timer starts from when you send your first request in a session, not at a fixed time like "midnight" or "every hour on the hour"
- **No calendar synchronization**: There's no fixed reset time; each user's quota window is independent and continuous
- **Shared across all Claude surfaces**: Pro and Max plan usage counts toward the same quota on claude.ai, Claude Code, and Claude Desktop

### What This Means:

If you exhaust your quota at 2:30 PM, your new allocation becomes available at 7:30 PM (5 hours later) — not at the next calendar hour or midnight. The reset is individual to each user's activity pattern.

---

### Sources:
- [Using Claude Code with your Pro or Max plan](https://support.claude.com/en/articles/11145838-using-claude-code-with-your-pro-or-max-plan)
- [Manage extra usage for paid Claude plans](https://support.claude.com/en/articles/12429409-manage-extra-usage-for-paid-claude-plans)
- [How do usage and length limits work?](https://support.claude.com/en/articles/11647753-how-do-usage-and-length-limits-work)

1	---
2	name: Rockybot Architecture Reference
3	description: Full codebase map for rockybot — services, data model, watchers, Quartz/notes-web, key file paths. Read this at session start instead of re-exploring.
4	type: reference
5	originSessionId: 8b6934c1-4c6d-4f87-962d-95658ad400d5
6	---
7	## Rockybot — Architecture Reference
8	
9	**Repo:** `/home/ubuntu/src/rockybot`  
10	**Version (as of last update):** 1.0.1 → next: 1.1.0 (unreleased on dev)
11	
12	---
13	
14	### Top-Level Structure
15	
16	```
17	/services/bot/            Main polling bot + all 5 watchers
18	/services/obsidian-bridge/  Vault file watcher → Quartz static site builder
19	/services/obsidian-headless/  Obsidian Sync daemon (remote deployments only)
20	/vault-scaffold/          Default vault structure + prompt templates (baked into bot image)
21	/docs/                    User-facing documentation
22	/tests/                   Unit + integration tests
23	/scripts/release.sh       Semver bump + CHANGELOG + git tag automation
24	docker-compose.yml        Production 4-service stack
25	docker-compose.dev.yml    Dev stack (single container, temp vault, tight budgets)
26	.env.example              All env vars with defaults
27	```
28	
29	---
30	
31	### Data Model
32	
33	**Storage:** Markdown files with YAML frontmatter — no database. Vault directory is the single source of truth.
34	
35	**Standard frontmatter fields:**
36	```yaml
37	tags: [research-request]
38	created: 2026-04-17
39	status: pending|processing|completed|error|awaiting-input
40	updated: 2026-04-17
41	sources:
42	  - url: https://...
43	    title: "Source Title"
44	related: ["[[wikilink]]", "[[other]]"]
45	publish: true|false   # Quartz publication gate
46	```
47	
48	**Research topic layout:**
49	```
50	research/<topic>/
51	├── index.md            Overview, TL;DR, sub-page list (publish: true here gates Quartz)
52	├── options-comparison.md
53	├── tradeoffs.md
54	└── ...other sub-pages
55	```
56	
57	**Note-to-note links:** Obsidian wikilink format `[[research/topic/page|display text]]` — file-path relative to vault root. No separate ID system.
58	
59	**Callout syntax (inline task triggers):**
60	```
61	> [!claude]    → amend-watcher: targeted inline edit
62	> [!expand]    → expand-watcher: create new sub-page
63	> [!revise]    → revise-watcher: corpus-wide rewrite of topic
64	```
65	
66	---
67	
68	### Services / Docker Stack
69	
70	| Service | Image | Role | Port |
71	|---|---|---|---|
72	| `rockybot` | `ghcr.io/mrwong/rockybot` | Main polling bot, all Claude tasks | internal |
73	| `rockybot-bridge` | `ghcr.io/mrwong/rockybot-bridge` | Vault watcher → Quartz build | internal |
74	| `notes-web` | `nginx:alpine` | Serves built Quartz static site | 8080 |
75	| `rockybot-headless` | `ghcr.io/mrwong/rockybot-headless` | Obsidian Sync daemon (remote only) | internal |
76	
77	**Shared volumes:**
78	- `./data/vault:/vault` — vault (shared between bot, bridge, headless)
79	- `./data/quartz-output:/quartz-output` — built site (shared between bridge, notes-web)
80	
81	---
82	
83	### Bot Watchers
84	
85	All watchers serialized by global mutex in `index.js`. Serial execution order: inbox → amend → expand → revise → lint.
86	
87	| Watcher | File | Trigger | Budget | Model |
88	|---|---|---|---|---|
89	| **inbox** | `inbox-watcher.js` | `research/inbox/*.md` with `status: pending` | $2.00 | sonnet |
90	| **amend** | `amend-watcher.js` | `> [!claude]` callout anywhere | $0.50 | haiku |
91	| **expand** | `expand-watcher.js` | `> [!expand]` callout | $1.00 | sonnet |
92	| **revise** | `revise-watcher.js` | `> [!revise]` in topic `index.md` | $4.00 | sonnet |
93	| **lint** | `lint-watcher.js` | `status: pending` in `research/lint-trigger.md` | $5.00 | sonnet |
94	
95	Inbox watcher runs a **Karpathy ingest pass** as its final step: Claude re-reads existing research tree and adds backlinks from new topic to related existing pages.
96	
97	---
98	
99	### Claude Integration
100	
101	**No REST API.** Claude invoked as subprocess via `claude --print <prompt>`.
102	
103	- **Runner:** `services/bot/src/claude-runner.js` — spawns CLI, collects both stdout+stderr, never rejects
104	- **Billing:** `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` (default) or `CLAUDE_SUBSCRIPTION_MODE=true` (subscription first, API fallback)
105	- **Error classification:** `classifyOutput(stderr, stdout)` — checks BOTH streams; patterns for auth errors and rate limits. Critical: Claude emits usage-limit messages on stdout ("You've hit your limit · resets 7am (UTC)"), not stderr.
106	- **Interactive auth:** If subscription session expires and `DISCORD_INTERACTIVE_AUTH=true`, posts OAuth URL to Discord as button; `DISCORD_AUTH_TIMEOUT_MINUTES` (default 5) before fallback
107	- **Interactive rate-limit hold:** If subscription usage limit is hit and `DISCORD_INTERACTIVE_AUTH=true`, posts Discord buttons ("Wait for reset" / "Use API Key"); holds mutex indefinitely (up to 24h) waiting for user choice or reset time. Parses reset time from error message ("resets 7am (UTC)") for precise sleep. Webhook-only mode auto-waits silently.
108	- **Budget:** `--max-budget-usd` flag (API-key mode only; subscription mode ignores budget flag)
109	- **Prompts:** Loaded from vault `research/*-prompt.md`; user-editable in Obsidian; hardcoded fallbacks if missing
110	
111	---
112	
113	### Topic Export
114	
115	Published topics can be downloaded as self-contained HTML ZIPs from the notes-web UI.
116	
117	**Flow:** Browser clicks "⬇ Export ZIP" on root index → `GET /export/<slug>` → nginx proxies to bridge:3001 → bridge validates, generates ZIP in memory, streams download.
118	
119	**Security gates (in order):**
120	1. Slug format: must match `/^[a-z0-9-]+$/` — blocks path traversal before any filesystem access
121	2. Publish whitelist: `getPublishedTopics(vaultPath)` called fresh on every request (never cached); slug must be in published list
122	3. Quartz output must exist (`quartzOutput/<slug>/` dir)
123	4. Concurrency guard: 429 if already building same topic
124	
125	**New files:**
126	- `services/obsidian-bridge/src/export-builder.js` — ZIP generation: walks topic HTML, rewrites paths via cheerio (static assets → relative, within-topic links → relative, cross-topic links → neutered with tooltip), includes Quartz `static/` dir
127	- `services/obsidian-bridge/src/http-server.js` — Node `http` server on port 3001 with all security gates; calls `buildTopicExport` on valid requests
128	
129	**New dependencies:** `archiver@^7`, `cheerio@^1` in `obsidian-bridge/package.json`
130	
131	**nginx:** `/export/` location proxies to `http://rockybot-bridge:3001/export/`; bridge exposes port 3001 internally (`expose: ["3001"]` in docker-compose)
132	
133	---
134	
135	### Notes-Web / Quartz Frontend
136	
137	**Not a dynamic web app.** Pre-rendered static HTML by Quartz v4.4.0.
138	
139	- **Bridge** (`services/obsidian-bridge/src/`) watches vault with chokidar (5s poll, 3s write-debounce), runs Quartz build on change
140	- **Publish gate:** Only topics with `publish: true` in their `index.md` are included in build
141	- **Link rendering:** Wikilinks `[[topic/page]]` → relative HTML links `<a href="/topic/page/">`
142	- **Features:** Full-text search, backlink graph, sidebar navigation (Quartz defaults)
143	- **Explorer navigation:** Bridge startup patches `quartz.layout.ts` at runtime to set `folderClickBehavior: "link"` so clicking a topic folder navigates to its index page (not just toggle)
144	- **nginx config:** `services/obsidian-bridge/nginx.conf`, serves `/quartz-output/` on port 8080, 7-day cache on static assets; rewrites `/research/*` → `/*` to fix vault-absolute wikilinks; proxies `/export/*` → bridge port 3001
145	
146	---
147	
148	### Key File Paths
149	
150	**Bot core:**
151	- `services/bot/src/index.js` — main loop, global mutex, watcher orchestration
152	- `services/bot/src/claude-runner.js` — Claude subprocess + auth fallback
153	- `services/bot/src/notifier.js` — Discord/Twilio notifications
154	- `services/bot/src/discord-bot.js` — Discord WebSocket bot (interactive auth)
155	- `services/bot/src/login-runner.js` — `claude login` OAuth URL capture
156	
157	**Watchers:**
158	- `services/bot/src/inbox-watcher.js`
159	- `services/bot/src/amend-watcher.js`
160	- `services/bot/src/expand-watcher.js`
161	- `services/bot/src/revise-watcher.js`
162	- `services/bot/src/lint-watcher.js`
163	
164	**Bridge:**
165	- `services/obsidian-bridge/src/index.js` — chokidar watcher setup, runtime quartz.layout.ts patch
166	- `services/obsidian-bridge/src/quartz-builder.js` — Quartz build + publish gate (`getPublishedTopics`)
167	- `services/obsidian-bridge/src/export-builder.js` — ZIP generation + HTML rewriting via cheerio
168	- `services/obsidian-bridge/src/http-server.js` — Node http server on :3001 with all security gates
169	- `services/obsidian-bridge/nginx.conf` — static site + /research/ redirect + /export/ proxy
170	
171	**Tests:**
172	- `Dockerfile.test` / `docker-compose.test.yml` — pre-baked test image (node:20-slim + unzip + npm ci); run with `npm run test:integration:docker`
173	- `tests/integration/quartz-builder.test.js` — getPublishedTopics
174	- `tests/integration/export-builder.test.js` — rewriteHtml + buildTopicExport ZIP structure
175	- `tests/integration/http-server.test.js` — HTTP security gates (49 tests total, no Claude needed)
176	
177	**Vault scaffold (user-editable prompts):**
178	- `vault-scaffold/research/research-prompt.md`
179	- `vault-scaffold/research/amend-prompt.md`
180	- `vault-scaffold/research/expand-prompt.md`
181	- `vault-scaffold/research/revise-prompt.md`
182	- `vault-scaffold/research/lint-prompt.md`
183	
184	**Docs (human-facing):**
185	- `docs/INSTALLATION.md`, `docs/WORKFLOW.md`, `docs/CONFIGURATION.md`, `docs/NOTIFICATIONS.md`, `docs/DEVELOPMENT.md`
186	- `CHANGELOG.md`
187	
188	---
189	
190	### Release Process
191	
192	1. `./scripts/release.sh` on `main` — shows commits since last tag, proposes semver bump
193	2. User confirms → script updates `services/bot/package.json` version + `CHANGELOG.md`, creates git tag
194	3. Push tag → GitHub Actions builds & pushes images to GHCR
195	4. Image tags: `latest`, `vX.Y.Z`, `vX.Y`
196	5. `dev` branch → `edge`/`dev` images (pre-release)
197	
198	**Conventional commits:** `feat:` → minor, `fix:` → patch, `feat!:`/`fix!:` → major, `docs:`/`chore:`/`test:` → no bump
199	
200	---
201	
202	### Key Design Decisions
203	
204	- **No database:** Vault files are source of truth; direct Obsidian access
205	- **Global mutex:** Prevents concurrent Claude runs + vault conflicts
206	- **File-based state:** Status in frontmatter survives restarts/crashes
207	- **Prompts in vault:** User-customizable without redeploying
208	- **Subscription + API fallback:** Layer budget strategies
209	- **Quartz static site:** Zero-cost hosting; opt-in publish flag per topic
210	- **Obsidian Sync (not LiveSync):** LiveSync conflict resolution breaks status frontmatter fields
211

### Bot Watchers

| Watcher | File | Trigger | Budget | Model |
|---|---|---|---|---|

**Security gates (in order):**
1. Slug format: must match `/^[a-z0-9-]+$/` — blocks path traversal before any filesystem access
2. Publish whitelist: `getPublishedTopics(vaultPath)` called fresh on every request (never cached); slug must be in published list
3. Quartz output must exist (`quartzOutput/<slug>/` dir)
4. Concurrency guard: 429 if already building same topic

**New files:**
- `services/obsidian-bridge/src/export-builder.js` — ZIP generation: walks topic HTML, rewrites paths via cheerio (static assets → relative, within-topic links → relative, cross-topic links → neutered with tooltip), includes Quartz `static/` dir
- `services/obsidian-bridge/src/http-server.js` — Node `http` server on port 3001 with all security gates; calls `buildTopicExport` on valid requests

**New dependencies:** `archiver@^7`, `cheerio@^1` in `obsidian-bridge/package.json`

**nginx:** `/export/` location proxies to `http://rockybot-bridge:3001/export/`; bridge exposes port 3001 internally (`expose: ["3001"]` in docker-compose)

---

### Notes-Web / Quartz Frontend

**Not a dynamic web app.** Pre-rendered static HTML by Quartz v4.4.0.

- **Bridge** (`services/obsidian-bridge/src/`) watches vault with chokidar (5s poll, 3s write-debounce), runs Quartz build on change
- **Publish gate:** Only topics with `publish: true` in their `index.md` are included in build
- **Link rendering:** Wikilinks `[[topic/page]]` → relative HTML links `<a href="/topic/page/">`
- **Features:** Full-text search, backlink graph, sidebar navigation (Quartz defaults)
- **Explorer navigation:** Bridge startup patches `quartz.layout.ts` at runtime to set `folderClickBehavior: "link"` so clicking a topic folder navigates to its index page (not just toggle)
- **nginx config:** `services/obsidian-bridge/nginx.conf`, serves `/quartz-output/` on port 8080, 7-day cache on static assets; rewrites `/research/*` → `/*` to fix vault-absolute wikilinks; proxies `/export/*` → bridge port 3001

---

### Key File Paths

**Bot core:**
- `services/bot/src/index.js` — main loop, global mutex, watcher orchestration
- `services/bot/src/claude-runner.js` — Claude subprocess + auth fallback
- `services/bot/src/notifier.js` — Discord/Twilio notifications
- `services/bot/src/discord-bot.js` — Discord WebSocket bot (interactive auth)
- `services/bot/src/login-runner.js` — `claude login` OAuth URL capture

**Watchers:**
- `services/bot/src/inbox-watcher.js`
- `services/bot/src/amend-watcher.js`
- `services/bot/src/expand-watcher.js`
- `services/bot/src/revise-watcher.js`
- `services/bot/src/lint-watcher.js`

**Bridge:**
- `services/obsidian-bridge/src/index.js` — chokidar watcher setup, runtime quartz.layout.ts patch
- `services/obsidian-bridge/src/quartz-builder.js` — Quartz build + publish gate (`getPublishedTopics`)
- `services/obsidian-bridge/src/export-builder.js` — ZIP generation + HTML rewriting via cheerio
- `services/obsidian-bridge/src/http-server.js` — Node http server on :3001 with all security gates
- `services/obsidian-bridge/nginx.conf` — static site + /research/ redirect + /export/ proxy

**Tests:**
- `Dockerfile.test` / `docker-compose.test.yml` — pre-baked test image (node:20-slim + unzip + npm ci); run with `npm run test:integration:docker`
- `tests/integration/quartz-builder.test.js` — getPublishedTopics
- `tests/integration/export-builder.test.js` — rewriteHtml + buildTopicExport ZIP structure
- `tests/integration/http-server.test.js` — HTTP security gates (49 tests total, no Claude needed)

**Vault scaffold (user-editable prompts):**
- `vault-scaffold/research/research-prompt.md`
- `vault-scaffold/research/amend-prompt.md`
- `vault-scaffold/research/expand-prompt.md`
- `vault-scaffold/research/revise-prompt.md`
- `vault-scaffold/research/lint-prompt.md`

**Docs (human-facing):**
- `docs/INSTALLATION.md`, `docs/WORKFLOW.md`, `docs/CONFIGURATION.md`, `docs/NOTIFICATIONS.md`, `docs/DEVELOPMENT.md`
- `CHANGELOG.md`

---

### Release Process

1. `./scripts/release.sh` on `main` — shows commits since last tag, proposes semver bump
2. User confirms → script updates `services/bot/package.json` version + `CHANGELOG.md`, creates git tag
3. Push tag → GitHub Actions builds & pushes images to GHCR
4. Image tags: `latest`, `vX.Y.Z`, `vX.Y`
5. `dev` branch → `edge`/`dev` images (pre-release)

**Conventional commits:** `feat:` → minor, `fix:` → patch, `feat!:`/`fix!:` → major, `docs:`/`chore:`/`test:` → no bump

---

### Key Design Decisions

- **No database:** Vault files are source of truth; direct Obsidian access
- **Global mutex:** Prevents concurrent Claude runs + vault conflicts
- **File-based state:** Status in frontmatter survives restarts/crashes
- **Prompts in vault:** User-customizable without redeploying
- **Subscription + API fallback:** Layer budget strategies
- **Quartz static site:** Zero-cost hosting; opt-in publish flag per topic
- **Obsidian Sync (not LiveSync):** LiveSync conflict resolution breaks status frontmatter fields

lastPrompt: I see, i was looking at making a queuing system for rockybot, such that it would hold requests unless i had free quota left in my sub plan. much of the research i'm doing isn't time sensitive, so I di…

content.text:
This is well-trodden ground in the existing architecture. Key facts I see:

- Rockybot runs in `CLAUDE_SUBSCRIPTION_MODE=true` — same quota pool as your Claude Code sessions
- It already has **reactive** quota handling: on hitting the limit, it parses the reset time from stdout and holds the mutex or posts Discord buttons
- The global mutex already serializes all watcher work

The gap is **proactive** quota preservation — don't burn quota that might be needed for interactive work, rather than just stop after it's gone.

There are three approaches worth considering, each with different tradeoffs:

---

**Option A: Quiet-hours + pacing** (simplest, no quota awareness needed)

Add two env vars:
- `RESEARCH_QUIET_HOURS` (e.g., `09:00-18:00 UTC`) — suppress research during your work day
- `RESEARCH_MIN_INTERVAL_MINUTES` — enforce a floor between consecutive research completions

Queue items just accumulate during quiet hours and drain overnight. No quota tracking, no new state. Works because your interactive Claude Code usage is mostly daytime.

**Tradeoff:** blunt — if you have an evening session or a weekend work sprint, it doesn't adapt.

---

**Option B: Discord "hold" toggle** (low-friction manual control)

Add a Discord command (`!research hold` / `!research release`) that writes a `RESEARCH_PAUSED=true` sentinel to a vault file. The watcher loop checks it before each task.

**Tradeoff:** requires you to remember to toggle it. But since rockybot already has a Discord bot wired up, the implementation is small.

---

**Option C: Reactive probe before each task** (adaptive, quota-aware)

Before claiming the mutex for any research task, make a minimal sentinel Claude call (1-sentence prompt, very short). If it returns a quota error, hold the queue and retry after the parsed reset time. If it succeeds, the actual task runs using that same connection.

**Tradeoff:** each research task now costs a trivial probe token-burn before running, and adds ~2-5s latency. But it gives real-time quota status without any external state.

---

**My recommendation:** A + B combined.

Quiet hours handles the 90% case (overnight drain is fine for non-time-sensitive research). The Discord toggle gives you an escape hatch for "I'm about to do a heavy Claude session on a Sunday." The existing reactive hold remains the safety net if something slips through.

Option C is the most technically elegant but the probe calls themselves cost quota, which is ironic — and the latency adds up across a deep queue.

Want me to design the implementation for A+B?

Design an implementation plan for two features in the rockybot codebase at `/home/ubuntu/src/rockybot`. Read the relevant source files before designing the plan.

## Context

Rockybot is a homelab research bot. It polls a vault directory for research tasks (markdown files with YAML frontmatter) and runs them through Claude (invoked as a subprocess via `claude --print`). All watchers are serialized by a global mutex in `services/bot/src/index.js`. The bot runs under `CLAUDE_SUBSCRIPTION_MODE=true`, meaning it shares quota with the user's interactive Claude Code sessions.

**Key files to read:**
- `services/bot/src/index.js` — main loop, mutex, watcher orchestration
- `services/bot/src/claude-runner.js` — Claude subprocess + quota error handling
- `services/bot/src/discord-bot.js` — Discord WebSocket bot (interactive auth)
- `services/bot/src/notifier.js` — Discord/Twilio notifications
- `.env.example` — existing env vars

## Features to plan

### Feature A: Quiet hours + pacing

Add two env vars:
- `RESEARCH_QUIET_HOURS` — e.g., `"09:00-18:00"` in UTC. During this window, suppress research tasks (queue them up, drain outside the window). 
- `RESEARCH_MIN_INTERVAL_MINUTES` — enforce a minimum gap between consecutive research task completions. Prevents burst consumption of quota.

The check should happen before the watcher loop attempts to process any task. When inside quiet hours or within the pacing interval, the loop should skip processing (not sleep forever — the existing poll interval will naturally retry).

### Feature B: Discord "hold" toggle

Add a Discord command `!research hold` / `!research release` (or similar) that pauses/resumes research processing. State should be persisted to a small file in the vault (e.g., `research/.research-hold`) so it survives bot restarts.

The Discord bot already exists at `services/bot/src/discord-bot.js`. The user likely uses a specific Discord channel for bot interaction. The hold state should be visible — when the bot starts up and hold is active, it should log/notify.

## What the plan should include

- Which files to modify (with exact paths)
- New files to create, if any
- The logic flow for each feature
- Where exactly in the poll loop the gate checks should go
- How quiet hours parsing should work (edge cases: midnight-spanning windows like `22:00-06:00`)
- How the Discord command should be structured, given the existing discord-bot.js patterns
- Env var defaults and documentation in `.env.example`
- Any test considerations

Be specific. Include line-level guidance where it matters.

content.text:
Here's the full implementation plan:

---

## New file: `services/bot/src/research-gate.js`

Central module that owns all gate state and logic. Three checks, evaluated in priority order:

1. **Hold** — file-based (`research/.research-hold` in vault). `isHoldActive()` uses `fs.pathExistsSync`; `setHold(true/false)` creates/removes the file via `fs-extra`'s `outputFile`/`remove`.
2. **Quiet hours** — parsed once at module load from `RESEARCH_QUIET_HOURS` env var. Midnight-spanning windows (e.g. `22:00-06:00`) are handled: when `startMinutes >= endMinutes`, suppress if `now >= start OR now < end` instead of the normal `AND` check.
3. **Pacing** — in-memory `lastResearchCompletionMs` timestamp. Returns true if `Date.now() - last < MIN_INTERVAL_MS`.

Public API:
- `researchGateReason()` — returns a human-readable string if gated, or `null` if clear
- `recordResearchCompletion()` — called by inbox-watcher after any successful Claude run
- `setHold(bool)` / `isHoldActive()` — called by discord-bot

---

## Changes to existing files

**`index.js` (`pollAll`):** Gate wraps only the `scanInbox` call — amend/expand/revise/lint watchers are annotation-driven and should not be suppressed. On startup, if hold is active, log a warning and optionally notify via webhook.

```js
const gateReason = researchGateReason();
if (gateReason) {
  logger.info(`research inbox skipped — ${gateReason}`);
} else {
  await withGlobalLock('inbox', scanInbox);
}
// amend/expand/revise/lint proceed unconditionally
```

**`inbox-watcher.js`:** Call `recordResearchCompletion()` after any successful Claude run (even awaiting-input — Claude ran, quota was consumed).

**`discord-bot.js`:** Add `GuildMessages` + `MessageContent` intents (the latter is a privileged intent — requires toggling "Message Content Intent" in the Discord dev portal). Add a `messageCreate` listener for three commands: `!research hold`, `!research release`, `!research status`. The require of `research-gate` is done lazily inside the handlers to avoid circular dep risk at load time.

**`notifier.js`:** Add `notifyHoldActive()` — used by `main()` at startup when hold is already set (covers the "bot restarted, hold persisted" visibility case).

**`.env.example`:** Document both env vars with format and defaults.

---

## New file: `tests/unit/research-gate.test.js`

Pure logic — straightforward to unit test. Cover: normal and midnight-spanning quiet windows, boundary conditions (exactly at start/end minute), pacing before/after `recordResearchCompletion()`, `researchGateReason()` priority ordering, and unparseable `RESEARCH_QUIET_HOURS` (should warn once and disable, not crash). Use `jest.resetModules()` between env-variant tests, consistent with how `claude-runner.test.js` handles env mocking.

---

## Implementation order

1. `research-gate.js` — isolated, no dependencies on other new code
2. `.env.example` — docs only
3. `inbox-watcher.js` — one-line addition
4. `index.js` — gate check + startup notification
5. `notifier.js` — new export
6. `discord-bot.js` — most complex, last

---

Ready to implement?

Follow the `freshRequires()` / `jest.resetModules()` pattern established in `claude-runner.test.js`. Mock `fs-extra` for hold file operations.

```js
jest.mock('fs-extra', () => ({ existsSync: jest.fn(), outputFile: jest.fn(), remove: jest.fn() }));
jest.mock('../../services/bot/src/logger', () => ({ info: jest.fn(), warn: jest.fn() }));

function freshRequires(env = {}) {
  Object.assign(process.env, env);
  jest.resetModules();
  return { gate: require('../../services/bot/src/research-gate'), fs: require('fs-extra') };
}
```

**Quiet hours cases to cover:**
- Normal window (`09:00-18:00`): inside, outside, exactly at boundary start, exactly at end
- Midnight-spanning window (`22:00-06:00`): inside on both sides of midnight, outside in middle of day
- Equal start/end (`09:00-09:00`): treated as disabled, never gates
- Blank/missing: disabled
- Unparseable string: warns once, disabled (does not throw)

**Pacing cases:**
- `MIN_INTERVAL_MINUTES=0`: `isWithinPacingInterval()` always false
- Before `recordResearchCompletion()` called: always false (initial value 0 = never gated)
- After recording: true within interval, false after interval elapses (use `jest.useFakeTimers()`)

**Priority ordering:**
- `researchGateReason()` returns `'hold'` when all three are active
- Returns `'quiet_hours'` when hold inactive but in window
- Returns `'pacing'` when only pacing is active
- Returns `null` when nothing is active

**Hold file:**
- `isHoldActive()` returns `fs.existsSync()` result
- `setHold(true)` calls `fs.outputFile(HOLD_FILE, '')`
- `setHold(false)` calls `fs.remove(HOLD_FILE)`

**Notified items:**
- `markItemNotified` / `isItemNotified` / `clearNotifiedItems` round-trip

# rockybot configuration
# Copy this file to .env and fill in your values.
# All variables are optional unless marked REQUIRED.

# ─── Anthropic API (REQUIRED unless using Claude subscription) ───────────────
# Get your key at: https://console.anthropic.com/
REDACTED

# ─── Claude billing mode ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# false (default): use ANTHROPIC_API_KEY for pay-per-use billing
# true: try Claude Pro/Max subscription first, fall back to API key
# Subscription mode requires mounting ~/.claude into the container
CLAUDE_SUBSCRIPTION_MODE=false

# ─── Vault path ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Path inside the container where the Obsidian vault is mounted
# (matches the volume mount in docker-compose.yml)
VAULT_PATH=/vault

# ─── Domain name (for obsidian-bridge / Quartz site) ─────────────────────────
# The domain where notes-web will be accessible (e.g. notes.example.com)
# Only needed if using the full Obsidian stack. Defaults to localhost.
DOMAIN_NAME=localhost

# ─── Polling interval ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# How often (in seconds) to check the vault for new work. Default: 600 (10 min)
INBOX_POLL_SECONDS=600

# ─── Spending caps per watcher invocation ────────────────────────────────────
# Each watcher caps Claude spend per file/run. Adjust to your comfort level.
RESEARCH_BUDGET_USD=2.00
AMEND_BUDGET_USD=0.50
EXPAND_BUDGET_USD=1.00
REVISE_BUDGET_USD=4.00
LINT_BUDGET_USD=5.00

# ─── Model selection per watcher ─────────────────────────────────────────────
# haiku is cheaper; sonnet is more capable. Inbox always uses sonnet.
AMEND_MODEL=haiku
EXPAND_MODEL=sonnet
REVISE_MODEL=sonnet
LINT_MODEL=sonnet

# ─── Discord notifications (optional) ────────────────────────────────────────
# Create a webhook in your Discord server: Server Settings → Integrations → Webhooks
# Leave blank to disable Discord notifications.
DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL=

# ─── Interactive Discord auth (optional) ──────────────────────────────────────
# When DISCORD_INTERACTIVE_AUTH=true and CLAUDE_SUBSCRIPTION_MODE=true, auth
# failures pause the task and post a Discord message.  The bot spawns `claude login`
# headlessly, captures the OAuth URL, and sends it as a button so you can re-auth
# from Discord (phone, browser, anywhere) without SSH-ing into the host.
# After the timeout the task auto-falls-back to the API key.
#
# Requires a Discord bot token (not just a webhook):
#   1. discord.com/developers → New Application → Bot → Reset Token → copy token
#   2. OAuth2 → URL Generator: scope "bot", permissions: Send Messages + Read Message History
#   3. Add the bot to your server via the generated URL
#   4. Right-click your target channel → Copy Channel ID (enable Developer Mode first)
DISCORD_INTERACTIVE_AUTH=false
DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN=
DISCORD_CHANNEL_ID=
# Minutes to wait for user action before auto-falling back to API key. Default: 5
DISCORD_AUTH_TIMEOUT_MINUTES=5

# ─── WhatsApp notifications via Twilio (optional) ────────────────────────────
# Requires a Twilio account with the WhatsApp sandbox enabled.
# Leave all blank to disable WhatsApp notifications.
TWILIO_ACCOUNT_SID=
TWILIO_AUTH_TOKEN=
TWILIO_FROM=whatsapp:+14155238886
TWILIO_TO=whatsapp:+1XXXXXXXXXX

# ─── Development / testing ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Set to true to run watchers in dry-run mode (detects but does not call Claude)
DRY_RUN=false

# Changelog

All notable changes to rockybot are documented here. Version numbers follow [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/): `MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH`. Documentation-only changes do not increment the version.

## [1.1.1] — 2026-04-30

### Fixed

- **Subscription usage limit now notifies Discord and holds instead of silently falling back.** Previously, hitting the Claude Pro/Max daily usage limit caused the bot to silently switch to API key billing without any notification. Root cause: Claude CLI emits "You've hit your limit · resets 7am (UTC)" on **stdout** (not stderr), so the error classifier missed it entirely and routed the failure to the auth handler, which then failed trying to run an OAuth login flow against a rate-limit error.

  New behavior when `CLAUDE_SUBSCRIPTION_MODE=true` and the usage limit is hit:

  - **Interactive mode** (`DISCORD_INTERACTIVE_AUTH=true`): posts a Discord message with two buttons — **⏳ Wait for reset** and **💰 Use API Key**. Bot holds the mutex until the user responds. Clicking "Wait" causes the bot to sleep until the parsed reset time (e.g. "7am UTC"), then automatically retry subscription billing. Clicking "Use API Key" falls back to paid billing immediately.
  - **Webhook-only mode**: posts a Discord embed and auto-waits for the reset time without requiring user input.
  - **24h hard cap**: if still rate-limited after 24 hours, posts another notification and falls back to the API key.
  - Reset time is parsed directly from the CLI error message ("resets 7am (UTC)") so the bot sleeps to the exact reset moment.

## [1.1.0] — 2026-04-29

### Added

- **Topic export.** Published topics now have a "⬇ Export ZIP" link on the notes-web root index. Clicking it streams a self-contained ZIP containing all rendered HTML pages for the topic plus the Quartz CSS, JS, and font assets — fully usable offline. Cross-topic links are neutered and shown as plain text with a tooltip. The export server lives in the bridge container on port 3001, proxied through nginx. Security gates applied in order: slug format check (`[a-z0-9-]+` only), publish-list whitelist (re-read from vault on every request — unpublishing a topic blocks exports immediately), Quartz output existence check (503 if not yet built), and a per-topic concurrency guard (429 if already in progress).

- **Interactive Discord auth.** When `CLAUDE_SUBSCRIPTION_MODE=true` and subscription auth fails, the bot now pauses the failing task and posts a clickable OAuth button to Discord instead of immediately falling back to the API key. Completing the login from a phone or browser resumes the task under subscription billing. Configure with `DISCORD_INTERACTIVE_AUTH=true`, `DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN`, and `DISCORD_CHANNEL_ID`. A configurable timeout (`DISCORD_AUTH_TIMEOUT_MINUTES`, default 5) triggers automatic API key fallback if no one responds.

- **Version endpoint.** `GET /version` on the bridge (proxied at `https://notes.yourdomain/version`) returns `{"version":"x.y.z"}` baked from the image's `package.json`. Useful for verifying which image version is live after a deploy before smoke-testing.

- **Integration test suite** (50 tests, no Claude API key required). Covers the three major surface areas of obsidian-bridge: `getPublishedTopics` vault scanning and publish-flag filtering; `rewriteHtml`/`buildTopicExport` HTML rewriting and ZIP structure; and the HTTP export server's routing, slug format gate, publish whitelist, 503/429 guards, and `/version` endpoint. Run with `npm run test:integration:docker` — uses a pre-baked Docker image so no host Node.js install is needed.

### Fixed

- **notes-web: Explorer navigation.** Clicking a topic folder in the sidebar now navigates to the topic's index page instead of only toggling the expand/collapse state. Fix: bridge startup patches `quartz.layout.ts` at runtime to set `folderClickBehavior: "link"`.

- **notes-web: `/research/` 404s.** Vault notes use vault-absolute wikilinks that include a `research/` prefix in the rendered URL (e.g. `/research/my-topic/sub-page`). These returned 404 because the Quartz output tree doesn't include that prefix. Fix: nginx rewrites `/research/*` → `/*` before serving.

- **Export: lock held after client disconnect.** If a browser navigated away or timed out mid-download, the in-progress lock for that topic was never released, blocking all further exports until the bridge restarted. Fix: `req.on('close')` clears the lock immediately on disconnect.

- **Notifications: Discord webhook redirects.** Discord webhooks occasionally return a redirect. The `curl` call was not following redirects, silently dropping notifications. Fix: added `-L` flag.

## [1.0.1] — 2026-04-28

# Changelog

All notable changes to rockybot are documented here. Version numbers follow [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/): `MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH`. Documentation-only changes do not increment the version.

## [1.1.1] — 2026-04-30

### Fixed

- **Subscription usage limit now notifies Discord and holds instead of silently falling back.** Previously, hitting the Claude Pro/Max daily usage limit caused the bot to silently switch to API key billing without any notification. Root cause: Claude CLI emits "You've hit your limit · resets 7am (UTC)" on **stdout** (not stderr), so the error classifier missed it entirely and routed the failure to the auth handler, which then failed trying to run an OAuth login flow against a rate-limit error.

  New behavior when `CLAUDE_SUBSCRIPTION_MODE=true` and the usage limit is hit:

  - **Interactive mode** (`DISCORD_INTERACTIVE_AUTH=true`): posts a Discord message with two buttons — **⏳ Wait for reset** and **💰 Use API Key**. Bot holds the mutex until the user responds. Clicking "Wait" causes the bot to sleep until the parsed reset time (e.g. "7am UTC"), then automatically retry subscription billing. Clicking "Use API Key" falls back to paid billing immediately.
  - **Webhook-only mode**: posts a Discord embed and auto-waits for the reset time without requiring user input.
  - **24h hard cap**: if still rate-limited after 24 hours, posts another notification and falls back to the API key.
  - Reset time is parsed directly from the CLI error message ("resets 7am (UTC)") so the bot sleeps to the exact reset moment.

## [1.1.0] — 2026-04-29

### Added

- **Topic export.** Published topics now have a "⬇ Export ZIP" link on the notes-web root index. Clicking it streams a self-contained ZIP containing all rendered HTML pages for the topic plus the Quartz CSS, JS, and font assets — fully usable offline. Cross-topic links are neutered and shown as plain text with a tooltip. The export server lives in the bridge container on port 3001, proxied through nginx. Security gates applied in order: slug format check (`[a-z0-9-]+` only), publish-list whitelist (re-read from vault on every request — unpublishing a topic blocks exports immediately), Quartz output existence check (503 if not yet built), and a per-topic concurrency guard (429 if already in progress).

- **Interactive Discord auth.** When `CLAUDE_SUBSCRIPTION_MODE=true` and subscription auth fails, the bot now pauses the failing task and posts a clickable OAuth button to Discord instead of immediately falling back to the API key. Completing the login from a phone or browser resumes the task under subscription billing. Configure with `DISCORD_INTERACTIVE_AUTH=true`, `DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN`, and `DISCORD_CHANNEL_ID`. A configurable timeout (`DISCORD_AUTH_TIMEOUT_MINUTES`, default 5) triggers automatic API key fallback if no one responds.

- **Version endpoint.** `GET /version` on the bridge (proxied at `https://notes.yourdomain/version`) returns `{"version":"x.y.z"}` baked from the image's `package.json`. Useful for verifying which image version is live after a deploy before smoke-testing.

- **Integration test suite** (50 tests, no Claude API key required). Covers the three major surface areas of obsidian-bridge: `getPublishedTopics` vault scanning and publish-flag filtering; `rewriteHtml`/`buildTopicExport` HTML rewriting and ZIP structure; and the HTTP export server's routing, slug format gate, publish whitelist, 503/429 guards, and `/version` endpoint. Run with `npm run test:integration:docker` — uses a pre-baked Docker image so no host Node.js install is needed.

### Fixed

- **notes-web: Explorer navigation.** Clicking a topic folder in the sidebar now navigates to the topic's index page instead of only toggling the expand/collapse state. Fix: bridge startup patches `quartz.layout.ts` at runtime to set `folderClickBehavior: "link"`.

- **notes-web: `/research/` 404s.** Vault notes use vault-absolute wikilinks that include a `research/` prefix in the rendered URL (e.g. `/research/my-topic/sub-page`). These returned 404 because the Quartz output tree doesn't include that prefix. Fix: nginx rewrites `/research/*` → `/*` before serving.

- **Export: lock held after client disconnect.** If a browser navigated away or timed out mid-download, the in-progress lock for that topic was never released, blocking all further exports until the bridge restarted. Fix: `req.on('close')` clears the lock immediately on disconnect.

- **Notifications: Discord webhook redirects.** Discord webhooks occasionally return a redirect. The `curl` call was not following redirects, silently dropping notifications. Fix: added `-L` flag.

## [1.2.0] — 2026-05-02

### Added

- **Quiet hours gate.** Set `RESEARCH_QUIET_HOURS="HH:MM-HH:MM"` (UTC) to suppress inbox research during a time window — e.g. `09:00-18:00` to keep the subscription quota free for interactive Claude Code sessions during the work day. When a new item arrives during quiet hours, the bot sends a per-item Discord notification with an **▶️ Run now** button so you can promote individual items without lifting the gate for everything.

- **Per-item expedite.** Clicking the "Run now" button on a quiet-hours notification marks that specific item `status: expedited` in its frontmatter. Expedited items always run on the next poll regardless of quiet hours, hold, or pacing state — other queued items stay back. The button triggers an immediate poll so the item runs within seconds, not at the next poll interval.

- **Request pacing.** Set `RESEARCH_MIN_INTERVAL_MINUTES=N` to enforce a minimum gap between consecutive research task completions. Prevents quota bursts when several items are queued at once; each completion resets the timer.

- **Discord hold toggle.** Three new text commands available in your bot channel when `DISCORD_INTERACTIVE_AUTH=true`:
  - `!research hold` — pauses all inbox research (silent; other watchers keep running)
  - `!research release` — resumes inbox research
  - `!research status` — reports hold state and current gate reason

  Hold state is persisted to `research/.research-hold` in the vault, so it survives bot restarts. If the bot starts with hold active it logs a warning. Requires **Message Content Intent** enabled in the Discord developer portal.

- **New `expedited` frontmatter status.** Items marked `status: expedited` in `research/inbox/` are processed immediately regardless of gate state, then follow the normal completed/awaiting-input/error flow.

### Added

## [1.0.1] — 2026-04-28

### Fixed
- Claude auth silent failure when `HOME=/home/ubuntu` is set: the `node:20-alpine` base image runs as uid 1000 (`node`) but `/home/ubuntu` was owned by root, so Claude Code couldn't create lock files or write config updates. Both subscription billing and API key fallback would silently exit 0 with no output after a 30 s remote-settings timeout. Fix: create `/home/ubuntu` with `node:node` ownership in the Dockerfile.

## [1.0.0] — 2026-04-17

Initial public release extracted from a private homelab monorepo.

### Added
- Inbox watcher: drop a request into `research/inbox/`, Claude writes a multi-page wiki tree

- Expand watcher: `[!expand]` callout to create sub-pages
- Revise watcher: `[!revise]` callout for corpus-level rewrites across a topic's sub-pages
- Lint watcher: audit the full research wiki for orphaned pages and broken wikilinks
- Claude subscription billing mode with API key fallback
- Discord and Twilio WhatsApp notifications
- Obsidian Sync integration via headless container
- Quartz static site builder via obsidian-bridge container
- GitHub Actions CI: unit tests, integration tests, Docker image publish to GHCR

file.content:
Inbox watcher runs a **Karpathy ingest pass** as its final step: Claude re-reads existing research tree and adds backlinks from new topic to related existing pages.

---

### Claude Integration

**No REST API.** Claude invoked as subprocess via `claude --print <prompt>`.

- **Runner:** `services/bot/src/claude-runner.js` — spawns CLI, collects both stdout+stderr, never rejects
- **Billing:** `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` (default) or `CLAUDE_SUBSCRIPTION_MODE=true` (subscription first, API fallback)
- **Error classification:** `classifyOutput(stderr, stdout)` — checks BOTH streams; patterns for auth errors and rate limits. Critical: Claude emits usage-limit messages on stdout ("You've hit your limit · resets 7am (UTC)"), not stderr.
- **Interactive auth:** If subscription session expires and `DISCORD_INTERACTIVE_AUTH=true`, posts OAuth URL to Discord as button; `DISCORD_AUTH_TIMEOUT_MINUTES` (default 5) before fallback
- **Interactive rate-limit hold:** If subscription usage limit is hit and `DISCORD_INTERACTIVE_AUTH=true`, posts Discord buttons ("Wait for reset" / "Use API Key"); holds mutex indefinitely (up to 24h) waiting for user choice or reset time. Parses reset time from error message ("resets 7am (UTC)") for precise sleep. Webhook-only mode auto-waits silently.
- **Budget:** `--max-budget-usd` flag (API-key mode only; subscription mode ignores budget flag)
- **Prompts:** Loaded from vault `research/*-prompt.md`; user-editable in Obsidian; hardcoded fallbacks if missing

---

### Topic Export

Published topics can be downloaded as self-contained HTML ZIPs from the notes-web UI.

**Flow:** Browser clicks "⬇ Export ZIP" on root index → `GET /export/<slug>` → nginx proxies to bridge:3001 → bridge validates, generates ZIP in memory, streams download.

**Security gates (in order):**
1. Slug format: must match `/^[a-z0-9-]+$/` — blocks path traversal before any filesystem access
2. Publish whitelist: `getPublishedTopics(vaultPath)` called fresh on every request (never cached); slug must be in published list
3. Quartz output must exist (`quartzOutput/<slug>/` dir)
4. Concurrency guard: 429 if already building same topic

Parses reset time from error message ("resets 7am (UTC)") for precise sleep. Webhook-only mode auto-waits silently.
- **Budget:** `--max-budget-usd` flag (API-key mode only; subscription mode ignores budget flag)
- **Prompts:** Loaded from vault `research/*-prompt.md`; user-editable in Obsidian; hardcoded fallbacks if missing

### Quota Preservation (v1.2.0+)

**Module:** `services/bot/src/research-gate.js` — owns all gate state, checked inside `scanInbox`.

- `RESEARCH_QUIET_HOURS="HH:MM-HH:MM"` UTC — suppress inbox during window (midnight-spanning supported). Gate is **per-item**: new arrivals during quiet hours get a Discord notification with a per-item **▶️ Run now** button.
- `RESEARCH_MIN_INTERVAL_MINUTES=N` — minimum gap between consecutive completions. Pacing is in-memory; resets on restart.
- `!research hold/release/status` Discord text commands — persisted to `research/.research-hold` in vault (survives restart). Requires **Message Content Intent** in Discord dev portal.
- Gate priority: hold > quiet_hours > pacing. Gate only applies to `status: pending` items.
- `status: expedited` — new frontmatter status set by the expedite button handler; always runs regardless of gate state. Normal `pending` → `expedited` via `expediteItem(filename)` in inbox-watcher.
